title: Attention Mechanism and Transformers
summary: The architecture behind GPT, BERT, and all modern LLMs. Understand this deeply.
key_concepts: Self-attention: Q, K, V = XW_Q, XW_K, XW_V
Multi-head attention: parallel attention heads concatenated
Transformer block: attention + FFN + LayerNorm + residual
Encoder vs Decoder vs Encoder-Decoder architectures
exercises: Implement scaled dot-product attention from scratch in numpy.
Why do we divide by sqrt(d_k) in attention?
What is the time and space complexity of self-attention? (O(n^2 d)) — why is this a problem for long sequences?
code_example:
import numpy as np
import torch
import torch.nn as nn

# Scaled dot-product attention (numpy)
def attention(Q, K, V, mask=None):
    d_k = Q.shape[-1]
    scores = Q @ K.transpose(-2, -1) / np.sqrt(d_k)
    if mask is not None:
        scores = scores.masked_fill(mask == 0, -1e9)
    weights = np.softmax(scores, axis=-1)
    return weights @ V, weights

# Multi-head attention (PyTorch)
class MultiHeadAttention(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, d_model, n_heads):
        super().__init__()
        self.d_k = d_model // n_heads
        self.n_heads = n_heads
        self.W_Q = nn.Linear(d_model, d_model)
        self.W_K = nn.Linear(d_model, d_model)
        self.W_V = nn.Linear(d_model, d_model)
        self.W_O = nn.Linear(d_model, d_model)

    def forward(self, x, mask=None):
        B, T, D = x.shape
        Q = self.W_Q(x).view(B, T, self.n_heads, self.d_k).transpose(1,2)
        K = self.W_K(x).view(B, T, self.n_heads, self.d_k).transpose(1,2)
        V = self.W_V(x).view(B, T, self.n_heads, self.d_k).transpose(1,2)
        scores = (Q @ K.transpose(-2,-1)) / self.d_k**0.5
        weights = torch.softmax(scores, dim=-1)
        out = (weights @ V).transpose(1,2).contiguous().view(B, T, D)
        return self.W_O(out)
thinking_questions: Why can attention capture long-range dependencies better than RNNs?
What is the difference between self-attention and cross-attention?
